Restaurant.title: Restaurant
Restaurant.description: Represents a restaurant using the eMenu Digital Hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Restaurant entity.

contactEmail.description: The contact email address for the restaurant.

contactPhone.description: The contact phone number for the restaurant.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the eMenu Digital Hub, typically a restaurant owner or manager.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the restaurant (e.g., owner, manager).

ProductCategory.title: ProductCategory
ProductCategory.description: Represents a category of products in the eMenu.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the product category (e.g., Appetizers, Main Courses).

description.description: A description of the product category.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product offered in the eMenu.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

imageUrl.description: URL of the product image, can be null.

inventory.description: Current stock level of the product.

Promotion.title: Promotion
Promotion.description: Represents a promotion or coupon for menu items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Promotion entity.

discountType.description: The type of discount (e.g., percentage, fixed amount).

startDate.description: The start date of the promotion.

endDate.description: The end date of the promotion.

productIds.description: References to Products. (Relationship: Product N:N Promotion)

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents an order placed by a customer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

orderDate.description: The date and time the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the order.

customerName.description: The name of the customer who placed the order.

customerContact.description: The contact information of the customer (e.g., phone number).

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents an item within an order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the order item.

itemPrice.description: The price of the item at the time of the order.

Page.title: Page
Page.description: Represents a static page in the eMenu Digital Hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Page entity.

content.description: The HTML content of the page.

slug.description: The URL-friendly slug for the page.

isPublished.description: Indicates whether the page is published and visible.

definition.description: Stores restaurant information. Top-level entity, with all other entities scoped under it.
Stores user accounts associated with a restaurant. Path-based ownership ensures only the restaurant can manage its users.
Stores product categories for a restaurant. Path-based ownership.

params.name: productCategoryId
params.description: Unique identifier for the product category.

definition.description: Stores products for a restaurant. Path-based ownership.
Stores promotions for a restaurant. Path-based ownership.
Stores orders for a restaurant. Path-based ownership.
Stores order items for an order. Nested under orders for data locality.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the order item.

definition.description: Stores static pages for a restaurant. Path-based ownership.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure Authorization Independence, DBAC, and support secure list operations (QAPs). It prioritizes path-based ownership for private user data and uses denormalization via membership maps for collaborative data. The structure incorporates Structural Segregation by maintaining homogeneous security postures within collections.

Restaurants are the top-level entity. Each restaurant 'owns' users, products, categories, promotions, orders, order items, and pages. User documents are stored directly under the restaurant to simplify ownership-based security rules. This provides Authorization Independence as well as making it trivial to perform list operations for a specific restaurant.

Data like Products and ProductCategories are also nested under `/restaurants/{restaurantId}`. This simplifies filtering and listing products/categories specific to a restaurant. Orders and OrderItems follow a similar pattern, ensuring all relevant data for a restaurant's operations is easily accessible and manageable under their designated path.

Pages are also stored under each restaurant, facilitating easy management and retrieval of restaurant-specific content. The use of restaurantId ensures that all collections are properly scoped and secured.

Authentication is handled by Firebase Auth, with roles managed implicitly via user documents associated with each restaurant.
